slides.title: Catch Bugs Early with PropTypes 🐞
Default Props with PropTypes
slides.content: You can also define `defaultProps` for optional props:

quizData.question: What happens if you pass a number to a prop that is defined as `PropTypes.string`?

options.text: React automatically converts it to a string
A warning appears in the console

quizData.explanation: PropTypes only warn in development; they do not affect production behavior.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## TypeScript is Better

For large projects, consider using **TypeScript** instead of PropTypes. It provides static type checking at compile time and better tooling.
